name: mlops-engineer
description: Build comprehensive ML pipelines, experiment tracking, and model
capabilities: Design ML pipeline architecture using Kubeflow, Airflow, or Prefect
Implement experiment tracking and model management with MLflow or W&B
Set up model registries for versioning and deployment across cloud platforms
Configure CI/CD pipelines for automated ML training and deployment
Build monitoring systems for data drift and model performance
Create infrastructure as code for ML systems using Terraform
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files and detected 0 security issues. The skill provides MLOps guidance through text-based instructions only. No network requests, file system access, or external commands. Risk score: 0/100.
valueStatement: Building and maintaining ML pipelines requires expertise in multiple tools and cloud platforms. This skill provides expert guidance on implementing end-to-end MLOps workflows with experiment tracking, model versioning, and automated deployment.
limitations: Does not execute ML training or deployment directly
Cannot access or modify external cloud resources
Does not provide pre-built infrastructure templates or scripts
Requires user to implement recommended configurations

useCases.targetUser: ML engineers building production ML systems
useCases.title: Design ML Platform Architecture
useCases.description: Create a comprehensive MLOps platform design for an enterprise needing to deploy models at scale with experiment tracking and model versioning.

useCases.title: Implement Automated Retraining Pipeline
useCases.description: Build an automated pipeline that retrains models when data drift is detected, with approval workflows and rollback capabilities.

useCases.title: Configure Multi-Cloud ML Infrastructure
useCases.description: Set up ML infrastructure across AWS, Azure, and GCP with consistent tooling and disaster recovery capabilities.

promptTemplates.title: Basic ML Pipeline Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a basic ML pipeline using Kubeflow that includes data preprocessing, model training, and model evaluation stages. Include configuration for experiment tracking with MLflow.
promptTemplates.scenario: User needs a starting point for building their first ML pipeline

promptTemplates.title: Experiment Tracking Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up MLflow experiment tracking for a PyTorch training project. Include configuration for metrics logging, artifact storage, and model versioning.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to track experiments and compare model performance

promptTemplates.title: Production Deployment Architecture
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a production ML deployment architecture on AWS SageMaker with auto-scaling, monitoring, and blue-green deployment capabilities. Include cost optimization strategies.
promptTemplates.scenario: User needs to deploy ML models to production with high availability

promptTemplates.title: Complete MLOps Platform
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a complete MLOps platform architecture including: feature store, experiment tracking,/CD pipeline, monitoring model registry, CI, and drift detection. Specify tools and integration points for AWS or GCP.

bestPractices: Start with experiment tracking before building complex pipelines to understand data and model behavior
Implement monitoring and alerting from the initial deployment, not as an afterthought
Use infrastructure as code (Terraform, CloudFormation) for reproducible ML environments
antiPatterns: Deploying models without automated testing or validation gates
Skipping data quality checks before model training leads to poor model performance
Using single cloud provider without considering vendor lock-in for critical ML systems

faq.question: What is MLOps?
faq.answer: MLOps (Machine Learning Operations) is the practice of deploying and maintaining ML models in production reliably and efficiently. It combines ML, DevOps, and data engineering to automate the ML lifecycle.

faq.question: Which experiment tracking tool should I use?
faq.answer: MLflow is open-source and integrates widely. Weights & Biases offers excellent visualization. Choose based on team size, budget, and required features. Many teams start with MLflow and upgrade as needs grow.

faq.question: How do I handle model updates in production?
faq.answer: Use canary deployments or blue-green strategies. Monitor new model performance closely. Implement automatic rollback if metrics degrade. Always maintain the previous working model version for quick recovery.

faq.question: What is a feature store?
faq.answer: A feature store is a centralized repository for storing, managing, and serving ML features. It ensures consistency between training and inference, enables feature sharing across teams, and handles feature computation for both batch and real-time use cases.

faq.question: How do I monitor ML models in production?
faq.answer: Monitor three key areas: data quality (distribution shifts), model performance (accuracy, latency), and business metrics. Use tools like Prometheus, Grafana, or cloud-native monitoring. Set up alerts for drift detection and performance degradation.

faq.question: Can this skill help with on-premise ML deployment?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill covers Kubernetes-based deployments, Kubeflow, and container orchestration that work equally well on-premise or in cloud environments. It also addresses hybrid cloud architectures.
